danger_signals.visual_form: Zombies, ghouls, skeletal creatures, Red Arremers, Belials, Fire Killers, swinging blades, gas streams, Werewolves, Skull Flowers, Blue Killers
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate combat threat; loss of armor or life if contact made

danger_signals.visual_form: Arthur stripped to underpants after first hit
danger_signals.meaning: One hit from any threat results in death; critical vulnerability state

danger_signals.visual_form: Earthquakes causing earth displacement, blizzards, heavy sea waves, avalanches, crumbling walls
danger_signals.meaning: Passive world-state threats that injure or kill regardless of enemy interaction

danger_signals.visual_form: Hidden treasure chests that contain deadly traps
danger_signals.meaning: Risk-reward mechanic; visual uncertainty masks lethal consequences

danger_signals.visual_form: Magician effect transforming Arthur into baby, girl, seal, or wasp form
danger_signals.meaning: Temporary debilitation; loss of combat capability and armor protection

danger_signals.visual_form: Boss entities protecting gates between stages
danger_signals.meaning: Stage-gating mechanic; must be defeated to progress
Pressure state; failure to defeat Foul Guardian within time results in death or quest restart

safety_signals.visual_form: Knight in full plate armor with lance
safety_signals.meaning: Damage buffer; can absorb one hit before losing armor
Damage buffer + Enchanted Weapon upgrade; stronger projectile output
Maximum protection state; includes Moon Shield (blocks one projectile while standing still) and rechargeable magic attack
Tactical tool variation; some weapons provide speed advantage or area effect
Quest objective marker; indicates story progression and unlocks critical knowledge (Goddess's Bracelet requirement)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Steel/Bronze/Gold Knight armor with lance; shield heraldry implied
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Humanoid protagonist in armor; transforms to weakened underpants state or magically-cursed forms (baby, girl, seal, wasp)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Graveyards, abandoned ships, caverns, ice/snow zones, royal castle interior (Sardius's domain, conquered kingdom spaces)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Emperor Sardius throne; Phantom Zone dominion; undead/supernatural aesthetic
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Diverse undead creatures (zombies, skeletons, ghouls, werewolves, fire demons); mechanical traps and environmental hazards
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: The Castle of the Emperor, Hallway of Ghouls, The Throne Room; rotting sea vessels; flame-filled caverns; ice/death-themed locations
Phantom Zone (kidnapped location); appears in narrative conclusion sequences

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Varied natural/supernatural environments: graveyards, seas, caverns, snow regions—all containing ghoul infestations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Arthur progresses through eight quests (Quests 1-8) defeating Foul Guardians; reaches final confrontation with Sardius but cannot defeat him

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Widespread enemy spawning; environmental hazards manifest (earthquakes, blizzards, waves, avalanches); castle domains darkened/corrupted
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Sardius has kidnapped Guinevere; enemies actively hunt Arthur; time pressure on quest completion

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Same environments repeated but with narrative context of artifact possession
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Arthur repeats quests 1-8 to reach Sardius again; now has mythical weapon required to defeat final boss; post-game victory sequence unlocks true ending

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Arthur reduced to underpants; vulnerability animation; knockback on hit
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: One additional hit results in life loss; heightened danger pressure; visual tells player critical health status

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Arthur morphs into baby, girl, seal, or wasp—each with distinct sprite and movement animation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Movement speed reduced; jumping capability altered; combat options eliminated; temporary helplessness until curse wears

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy spawn counts reduced (fewer Skull Flowers, Belials, Fire Killers, etc.); some obstacle patterns removed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Difficulty reduced; performance optimization; religious imagery censored (crosses → ankhs, Samael → Sardius)

environmental_storytelling.example: Quest stages named 'The Dead Place,' 'The Rotting Sea,' 'Vermilion Horror,' 'The Ghoul's Stomach,' 'The Deep Chill,' 'The Castle of the Emperor,' 'Hallway of Ghouls,' 'The Throne Room'—each name telegraphs both thematic danger and narrative progression toward Sardius's seat of power

First five quests traverse hostile natural/corrupted realms; final three quests move into Sardius's castle interior—visual geography mirrors narrative movement from invaded kingdom to enemy stronghold
Goddess's Bracelet must be found in second playthrough; its absence in first playthrough signals incomplete victory and Arthur's inability to harm the final boss—object narrative > dialogue
Steel → stripped state → death; visual progression communicates fragility and one-hit-death vulnerability without text; reinforces castle/ghoul threat severity

environmental_storytelling.technique: Enemy creature variety as ecosystem depth
environmental_storytelling.example: Distinct enemy types (zombies, fire demons, werewolves, blade traps, gas vents) suggest populated, layered Ghoul Realm with different hazard categories rather than generic 'enemies'

environmental_storytelling.technique: Rotating palace mechanic as architectural threat
environmental_storytelling.example: Level 4-2 features rotating palace structure (reference to Nebulus); environment itself becomes active antagonist, communicating Emperor's magical dominion over space

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Standard movement speed; can use equipped weapon; can double-jump; takes one hit before armor loss
Still mobile but next hit causes immediate death; visual tells indicate critical vulnerability; knockback effect on contact
Movement speed reduced or altered; jump height/mechanics changed; weapon functionality lost; temporary defenselessness until effect wears
Enhanced combat capability; shield blocks one projectile (only while standing still—requires deliberate positioning); magic attack available (rechargeable area effect)
Melee pursuit; slow movement; standard enemy encounter behavior; no special tells visible
Aggressive projectile fire or melee attacks; removal in European version suggests performance-critical encounters
Heightened speed/aggression compared to zombies; removal in European version indicates difficulty spike
Time-limited encounter; must be defeated within quest timer; gate-locking behavior signals progression dependency
Unkillable; battle results in narrative redirect; Guinevere appears to explain bracelet requirement
Defeatable; victory triggers true ending sequence; demonstrates artifact necessity through mechanical gate
Transforms Arthur into weakened form; temporary debilitation; visual morph communicates powerlessness

notes: Super Ghouls 'n Ghosts uses minimal textual exposition; world-state and danger communicate primarily through enemy spawning patterns, environmental hazards, visual degradation (armor loss), and location naming. Regional differences reveal censorship of Christian imagery (crosses → ankhs, Samael → Sardius), reducing religious faction signals. The dual-playthrough structure (must beat game twice to reach true ending) communicates world-state progression through mechanical gating rather than environmental change—environments repeat but narrative context shifts with artifact discovery. Armor system and transformation curses are primary vectors for communicating vulnerability and risk escalation. No explicit faction flags beyond Arthur (player) vs. Sardius (antagonist) + kidnapped Guinevere (objective marker).
